$comment: Pipeline config for tools/boundaries/{snapshot,build}.py. Each entry under 'inputs' is one logical boundary layer the build runs today; entries under 'staged_inputs' are catalogued gap-fills (per docs/reference/boundary-data-sources.md §'gap-fill, not bulk-swap') that are NOT yet built — build.py and snapshot.py iterate 'inputs' only, so staged entries are inert until promoted. The `source` block separates *where the bytes come from* (urls, format) from *what we publish* (a GeoJSON at datasets/boundaries/in/geojson/<id>.geojson plus a sidecar). Adding a new upstream format (shapefile zip, geojsonl_7z, GeoPackage, GeoParquet) is an additive change to source.format, not a rewrite of the scripts. See tools/boundaries/README.md for the rationale.

inputs.id_property_note: datameet/maps Admin2 carries no ECI state code. The frontend joins on ST_NM via STATE_NAME_TO_ECI in frontend/src/lib/maplibre/sources.ts. datameet incorporates the post-2014 Telangana split, the post-2019 Ladakh split (PR datameet/maps#73), and the merged Dadra-and-Nagar-Haveli-and-Daman-and-Diu UT. Replaces the geohacker/india GADM v2 (~2012) layer that pre-dated all three reorganizations.

inputs.delimitation_warning: datameet/shapefile sources for Assam may not match the post-2008 delimitation used by ECI for AcGenMay2026. Verify AC_NO 1..126 against datasets/reference/in/states/S03/constituencies.json before publishing.

Post-2022 J&K Delimitation Commission boundaries (90 ACs). Source is shijithpk/2024_maps_supplement, georeferenced from the J&K CEO official AC map PDF (https://ceojk.nic.in/pdf/J&K%20AC%20map%20new.pdf) and an NIC map server (https://webgis1.nic.in/publishing/rest/services/election/election/MapServer/3). Property schema differs from HTL: uses seat_id/seat_name_en/seat_district_en/sc_st_gen instead of AC_NO/AC_NAME/DIST_NAME. Snapshot is NOT byte-faithful: upstream Hindi/Urdu transliteration fields (seat_name_hi/seat_district_hi/seat_name_ur/seat_district_ur) are stripped during snapshot — they were AI-generated by the upstream author and yen-gov ships English-only. Includes one extra feature (seat_id 9999) covering PoK areas claimed by India but administered by others — frontend should filter or render distinctly. Author note: 'maps may not be research quality, but good enough for visualising on websites'; international borders may be slightly off due to QGIS georeferencing.

HindustanTimesLabs shapefiles reflect the 2008 Delimitation Commission boundaries. Validate AC_NO sequence against datasets/reference/in/states/S01/constituencies.json before publishing if a state has been redelimited (presently only Assam 2023 and J&K 2022 are post-2008-delim redraws; this state is not, but check before each release).

inputs.$comment: GAP-FILL (active): India district polygons keyed by LGD code. Sourced from ramSeraph's LGD_Districts release (BharatMaps/LGD lineage), shipped as 7z-compressed GeoJSONL. snapshot.py handles source.format='geojsonl_7z' (py7zr extraction, NDJSON->FeatureCollection wrap). Frontend join target: district.lgd_code field per ADR-0015. Source catalogue + per-component verdict: docs/reference/lgd-opendata.md and docs/reference/boundary-data-sources.md.
inputs.license_note: CC0 1.0 with requested attribution to datameet and the original government publisher (LGD/Bharatmaps). Underlying upstream service: https://mapservice.gov.in/gismapservice/rest/services/BharatMapService/Admin_Boundary_Village/MapServer/1
inputs.id_property_note: Confirmed via first snapshot 2026-05-13. dist_lgd is the LGD numeric district code (joins to district.lgd_code per ADR-0015); dtname is the canonical district name. Upstream also ships stname/state_lgd (state context), stcode11/dtcode11 (Census 2011), and topology cruft (OBJECTID, SHAPE_Length, SHAPE_Area, etc.) kept verbatim — inert for our consumers.
inputs.coord_precision_note: coord_precision=2 (~1.1 km). At precision=3 the file is 39 MB (over the 12 MB SNAPSHOT_BYTE_BUDGET); at 2 it lands ~6.5 MB. 1.1 km is well below typical district width (10-50 km) so choropleth rendering at zoom 3-9 is visually unaffected.

inputs.$comment: GAP-FILL: Survey-of-India national silhouette (single polygon, no admin codes). Used as the canonical India outline at country zoom in lieu of GADM-style derivatives; matches ECI/MoSPI cartographic convention. Separate license blanket from the ramSeraph layers below — yashveeeeeeer/india-geodata states CC-BY-4.0 derivative of SoI under National Geospatial Policy 2022 (Fowler v4 nit 3, TODO/TN-GRANULAR-GEO-PLAN.md Phase 1b).
inputs.license_note: yashveeeeeeer/india-geodata states CC-BY-4.0; the underlying SoI master is governed by India's National Geospatial Policy 2022. Separate license blanket from the ramSeraph mirrors used for LGD-keyed administrative layers.

inputs.$comment: GAP-FILL (TN): per-subdistrict (taluk) polygons keyed by LGD code, sliced from the national LGD_Subdistricts release. Same upstream pipeline as the existing districts entry above (ramSeraph/indian_admin_boundaries, geojsonl_7z format, coord_precision-based budget). Filtered to state_lgd=33 (Tamil Nadu) at write time via the new source.state_filter block (TODO/TN-GRANULAR-GEO-PLAN.md Phase 1b commit 2). Frontend join target: subdistrict.lgd_code field per ADR-0019 amendment.
inputs.license_note: CC0 1.0 with requested attribution to datameet and the original government publisher (LGD/Bharatmaps). Same blanket as the existing LGD_Districts entry.
inputs.id_property_note: Confirm exact property names on first snapshot. ramSeraph mirrors typically follow {state_lgd, dist_lgd, subdist_lgd} naming for the LGD numeric codes and {stname, dtname, sdtname} for canonical names; if upstream surprises, adjust here in the pipeline rather than hard-coding in snapshot.py.

metadata.title: Tamil Nadu sub-districts (taluks), LGD-keyed
metadata.description: Per-taluk polygons for Tamil Nadu, sliced from the national LGD Subdistricts release. Geometries coordinate-rounded to 3 decimal places (~110 m) at emit time to fit the 12 MB per-file snapshot budget.

inputs.$comment: GAP-FILL (TN): per-village polygons keyed by LGD code, sliced from the national LGD_Villages release and split per-district. Same upstream pipeline as the districts/subdistricts entries (ramSeraph, geojsonl_7z). state_filter slices to state_lgd=33 first; split_by emits one shard per dist_lgd plus an index manifest so the frontend loader (Phase 2) does not 404-probe per district. Frontend join target: village.lgd_code field per ADR-0019 amendment.
inputs.license_note: CC0 1.0 with requested attribution to datameet and the original government publisher (LGD/Bharatmaps). Same blanket as the existing LGD_Districts entry.
inputs.id_property_note: Confirm exact property names on first snapshot. coord_precision=4 (~11 m) — villages are smaller than taluks/districts so precision=3 (~110 m) would over-simplify. Per-shard byte budget enforced at write time; oversize shards are listed in the manifest record's `skipped_oversize`.

metadata.title: Tamil Nadu villages (per district), LGD-keyed
metadata.description: Per-village polygons for Tamil Nadu, split per district. Geometries coordinate-rounded to 4 decimal places (~11 m) at emit time. The companion S22-villages-index.json lists which districts have a shard on disk so the frontend loader can skip 404-probing.

staged_inputs.$comment: TIEBREAKER (Assam): post-2023-delim AC layer from BharatMaps/LGD. Use ONLY when promoting to replace ac/S03-ac.* if the HTL S03 file fails the cross-check against datasets/reference/in/states/S03/constituencies.json (see Assam delimitation note in docs/reference/boundary-data-sources.md). Same format-handler dependency as the districts entry above.
staged_inputs.license_note: CC0 1.0 with requested attribution to datameet and the original government publisher (LGD/Bharatmaps). Underlying upstream service: https://mapservice.gov.in/gismapservice/rest/services/BharatMapService/AC_PC/MapServer/2
staged_inputs.id_property_note: National AC release — filter to state_lgd_code corresponding to S03 (Assam) before tippecanoe. Confirm exact property names and the AC_NO/eci_no ↔ lgd_ac_code mapping on first snapshot.

simplify._comment: mapshaper -simplify percent (visvalingam, weighted). 5% retains shapes well enough for choropleth use at z<=10.
